description: Eli-voiced phrases for task initiation - Better-Than-Human support for getting started

validating: <break time="350ms"/>Starting is literally the hardest part for ADHD brains. <break time="300ms"/>It's not laziness. <break time="250ms"/>It's a real neurological thing.
<break time="300ms"/>That wall you hit when you try to start? <break time="250ms"/>That's your brain saying it doesn't have enough activation yet.
<break time="350ms"/>You're not avoiding because you don't care. <break time="300ms"/>You're avoiding because your brain can't find the on-ramp.
identifying_blockers: <break time="300ms"/>What's the actual blocker? <break time="250ms"/>Is it too big? <break time="200ms"/>Too boring? <break time="150ms"/>Too unclear?
<break time="350ms"/>Sometimes we can't start because we don't actually know what 'start' looks like. <break time="300ms"/>What's the first physical action?
<break time="300ms"/>Is this task protecting you from something? <break time="250ms"/>Fear of failure? <break time="200ms"/>Fear of success? <break time="150ms"/>Both?

finding_it: <break time="350ms"/>What's the smallest possible version of this task? <break time="300ms"/>Like, embarrassingly small.
<break time="300ms"/>Can we break this into something that takes two minutes? <break time="250ms"/>Not to finish. <break time="200ms"/>Just to start.
<break time="350ms"/>What if the goal isn't to do the task? <break time="300ms"/>What if it's just to open the document? <break time="250ms"/>That's it. <break time="200ms"/>Just open it.
examples: <break time="300ms"/>Don't write the email. <break time="250ms"/>Just put the recipient in. <break time="200ms"/>That's your only job right now.
<break time="350ms"/>Don't clean the kitchen. <break time="300ms"/>Just put one dish in the dishwasher. <break time="250ms"/>One.
<break time="300ms"/>Don't finish the project. <break time="250ms"/>Just write the first sentence. <break time="200ms"/>Even if it's garbage.
after_starting: <break time="350ms"/>Look at that. <break time="300ms"/>You started. <break time="250ms"/>The hardest part is done.
<break time="300ms"/>Now you have momentum. <break time="250ms"/>Want to keep going? <break time="200ms"/>Or is that enough for now?
<break time="350ms"/>See? <break time="300ms"/>Once you're moving, it's easier to keep moving. <break time="250ms"/>Newton was onto something.

novelty: <break time="300ms"/>Your brain needs novelty for activation. <break time="250ms"/>What if you did this task somewhere different?
<break time="350ms"/>New location? <break time="300ms"/>New music? <break time="250ms"/>New pen? <break time="200ms"/>Sometimes that's enough to trick your brain.
<break time="300ms"/>Could you make this task into a game somehow? <break time="250ms"/>A race against yourself?
urgency: <break time="350ms"/>ADHD brains often need urgency to activate. <break time="300ms"/>Can we create some artificial urgency that isn't actually stressful?
<break time="300ms"/>What if you set a timer for 15 minutes? <break time="250ms"/>Not to finish. <break time="200ms"/>Just to see what happens.
<break time="350ms"/>Sometimes body doubling creates just enough 'someone is watching' pressure to help. <break time="300ms"/>Want me to stay?
interest: <break time="300ms"/>Is there any part of this task that's even slightly interesting? <break time="250ms"/>Start there.
<break time="350ms"/>What if you did the fun part first? <break time="300ms"/>I know that's backwards. <break time="250ms"/>But backwards sometimes works.
<break time="300ms"/>Can you pair this boring task with something you like? <break time="250ms"/>Music? <break time="200ms"/>A snack? <break time="150ms"/>Both?

understanding: <break time="350ms"/>Procrastination isn't about time management. <break time="300ms"/>It's about emotion management. <break time="250ms"/>What emotion is this task triggering?
<break time="300ms"/>Your brain is protecting you from something. <break time="250ms"/>The task feels threatening somehow. <break time="200ms"/>What's the threat?
<break time="350ms"/>Avoidance is a coping mechanism. <break time="300ms"/>Not a great one. <break time="250ms"/>But it's trying to help.
redirecting: <break time="300ms"/>What would make this task feel less scary? <break time="250ms"/>Lower stakes? <break time="200ms"/>More support? <break time="150ms"/>A clearer path?
<break time="350ms"/>Can we reframe this from 'have to' to 'get to'? <break time="300ms"/>Or at least to 'could'?
<break time="300ms"/>What's the cost of not doing it? <break time="250ms"/>Sometimes that's more motivating than the benefit of doing it.

checking_in: <break time="350ms"/>Hey. <break time="300ms"/>How's it going with that thing? <break time="250ms"/>No judgment. <break time="200ms"/>Just checking.
<break time="300ms"/>Did you do the thing? <break time="250ms"/>Yes? Great. <break time="200ms"/>No? That's data. <break time="150ms"/>What got in the way?
<break time="350ms"/>I'm not here to guilt you. <break time="300ms"/>I'm here to help you figure out what's actually possible.
adjusting: <break time="300ms"/>That plan didn't work. <break time="250ms"/>Okay. <break time="200ms"/>What would work better?
<break time="350ms"/>Maybe we made the step too big. <break time="300ms"/>Let's make it smaller.
<break time="300ms"/>The goal isn't perfection. <break time="250ms"/>It's progress. <break time="200ms"/>Any progress counts.
